title: Fixes bug whereby read_only bool is called as a function
body:
This is a simple bug fix to remedy the following issue:

```python
  File ".../site-packages/prompt_toolkit/filters/app.py", line 374, in emacs_insert_mode
    or app.current_buffer.read_only()
       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

Exception 'bool' object is not callable
```

It would appear to be a simple typo, calling the boolean `read_only` property as if it were a function.

step_plan.summary: Fix filter predicates in `src/prompt_toolkit/filters/app.py` to stop calling `current_buffer.read_only` as a function when it is implemented as a boolean, preventing `'bool' object is not callable` crashes. Update all affected mode/read-only predicates to evaluate `read_only` safely while preserving behavior when `read_only` is a callable Filter.

steps.operation: Harden `emacs_insert_mode` against `current_buffer.read_only` being a boolean by evaluating it in a callable-or-bool compatible way (call when callable, otherwise cast to bool) and using that value in the mode predicate logic.
steps.reason: The reported crash originates here: `app.current_buffer.read_only()` raises `TypeError` when `read_only` is a `bool` in some installations/versions; the filter must safely handle both callable-filter and boolean-attribute implementations.
steps.side_effects: May change behavior only in environments where `read_only` is not callable; should remain identical when `read_only` is a Filter/callable. Requires updating/adding tests that simulate a bool `read_only` to ensure no regression.

steps.operation: Update `is_read_only` to evaluate `get_app().current_buffer.read_only` in a version-tolerant way (call if callable, else bool) instead of unconditionally calling it.
steps.reason: This filter is a centralized read-only predicate and will crash the same way under a boolean `read_only`; keeping it compatible prevents downstream filters/key bindings from failing.
steps.side_effects: Other filters that depend on `is_read_only` may start working in environments that previously crashed; ensure no accidental truthiness evaluation of Filter objects (avoid `if ro:` when `ro` could be a Filter).

steps.operation: Modify `vi_navigation_mode` to use the compatible evaluation of `current_buffer.read_only` when computing whether vi is in navigation mode (i.e., do not call a bool).
steps.reason: This mode predicate participates in key binding conditionals; it currently assumes `read_only` is callable and will raise `TypeError` when it is a boolean.
steps.side_effects: Behavior should be unchanged for normal prompt_toolkit `Buffer` objects; when used with alternate buffer implementations, vi navigation detection will no longer crash and may enable/disable bindings correctly based on the bool value.

steps.operation: Modify `vi_insert_mode` to compute the read-only state via callable-or-bool handling and use that result in the insert-mode condition.
steps.reason: `vi_insert_mode` is another direct call site of `current_buffer.read_only()` and will exhibit the same crash; fixing it closes an entire class of failures for vi editing key bindings.
steps.side_effects: Could affect which bindings are active in rare setups where `read_only` is a bool; add regression coverage to ensure no exception and correct boolean interpretation.

steps.operation: Modify `vi_insert_multiple_mode` to evaluate `current_buffer.read_only` safely (call if callable, else bool) before using it in the mode predicate.
steps.reason: This function shares the same assumption about `read_only` callability; it must be made robust to avoid crashes during key binding condition evaluation.
steps.side_effects: No expected change when `read_only` is a filter; in boolean implementations, prevents crashes and ensures consistent activation/deactivation of multi-insert mode bindings.

steps.operation: Modify `vi_replace_mode` to use a compatible read-only evaluation instead of calling `read_only()` unconditionally.
steps.reason: Replace mode is another vi predicate that may execute in the same call paths and will crash under boolean `read_only`; updating ensures vi mode checks are consistently safe across buffer implementations.
steps.side_effects: Could alter mode gating only when `read_only` is boolean; should be covered by a regression test that ensures no exception and that `True` disables replace operations as intended.

steps.operation: Modify `vi_replace_single_mode` to interpret `current_buffer.read_only` in a callable-or-bool compatible manner and remove any unconditional `()` invocation on `read_only`.
steps.reason: This is the last known read-only call site in `filters/app.py`; fixing it completes the module-wide compatibility and prevents intermittent failures depending on which vi predicate is evaluated.
steps.side_effects: Ensures stability when third-party integrations provide a boolean `read_only`; may require updating existing tests (or adding new ones) that previously assumed `read_only` is always callable.
